name: components/code
description: One component for code blocks, diffs, and terminal transcripts, switched by data-ls-variant, with line-level hooks for highlight, add/del, and prompt/output emphasis.

dataAttributes.name: data-ls-variant
dataAttributes.description: Selects the code presentation: plain block, diff (add/del lines), or terminal transcript (prompt/output lines).

dataAttributes.name: data-ls-line
dataAttributes.description: Line-level emphasis: highlight draws an accent bar, add/del render diff lines with drawn +/- markers, prompt/output style terminal transcripts.

usage: Author as a <figure class="ls-code"> with a <figcaption class="ls-code__header"> (filename or command context) and the code inside .ls-code__body > pre.
Wrap each line in a .ls-code__line span; +/- diff markers and $ prompts are drawn by CSS, so do not type them into the source.
Keep excerpts short: highlight the 1-3 lines the narration is about instead of pasting whole files.

composition.itemCountGuidance: One code block per slide; pair it with notes rather than a second block.
composition.copyGuidance: Up to about 12 lines of at most 60 characters; trim with ellipsis comments instead of shrinking the font.
useWhen: an API or config excerpt is the teaching content
a before/after change reads best as a diff
a command sequence and its output tell the story
avoidWhen: the excerpt would exceed roughly 15 lines
the audience needs the concept, not the syntax
the content is data, not code

alternatives.when: the concept matters more than the syntax
a product screenshot of the tool

useCases: Walk through an API call with the key line highlighted.
Show a before/after change as a diff.
Present a terminal session with prompts and dimmed output.
